svg.title: Dimension Ladder and 54D Projection
svg.desc: Two-panel dimensional ladder showing the stage-to-dimension lift on the left and projected particle slices on the right.
svg.text: The same Wallington lift read as stage count, ambient dimension, and projected slice

text.tspan: The left panel climbs continuously from 2D to the current 55D runtime ceiling; the
right panel shows 54D as nested projected slices rather than a literal view from
Figure 3b. Dimension ladder from 2D through the current 55D runtime ceiling, with named manuscript rungs and a
projected-slice metaphor for the 54D particle. The left panel treats D = K + 1 as the governing lift rule; the
right panel makes explicit that a 54D object can only be shown as lower-dimensional projections or
cross-sections, not as a literal full view.

svg.text: Runtime ladder with D = K + 1 and named rungs from 2D through 55D
Projected slices only: cross-section metaphor, not a literal 54D view

g.text: The first one-mode tier above Bythos: one cycle, one stage, one visible sliver.
The first drawable donut: one extra lift turns the line into a visible torus.
The Clifford-style three-cycle rung where the quark correspondence appears.
Five stages recover the full primitive alphabet in one lifted runtime surface.
Ten cycles, ninety directed emanations, and the first wide interaction field.
The manuscript's requested high-dimensional particle view: same local rule, lifted fifty-two times beyond the visible torus.
Current hero/runtime ceiling for the 1..54 stage sweep; one more lift than the 54D focus particle.

svg.text: Wallington lift rule: dimension D = stages K + 1
Higher-dimensional structure is shown as nested 2D/3D slices so a reader can see the lift without pretending to see all 54 dimensions directly.
